title: Investing to Make Canada a Global Critical Minerals Superpower
content: Canadian government has announced up to $50 million in funding for 32 projects aimed at strengthening Canadaâ€™s critical minerals value chains, enhancing economic opportunities, job creation, and energy security while reducing reliance on authoritarian governments. Major projects include transmission and access road developments for uranium and lithium extraction, electrification of mining operations, low-emission graphite production, battery recycling technologies, Indigenous-led geological surveys and education initiatives, and enhanced mineral inventory analysis.The funding is distributed across key areas, including infrastructure development through the Critical Minerals Infrastructure Fund (CMIF), which supports clean energy and transportation projects, research and innovation via the Critical Minerals Research, Development & Demonstration (CMRDD) program.
author: Analysts Account
